tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function shows mildly elevated creatinine, suggesting possible early renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, elevated bilirubin, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible renal and/or hepatic dysfunction. Low urine specific gravity suggests impaired concentrating ability. HbA1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further evaluation for renal and hepatic pathology and correlation with clinical findings.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
